channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: HEBATRON: A Hebrew-Specialized Open-Weight Mixture-of-Experts Language Model
item.description: arXiv:2605.11255v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present Hebatron, a Hebrew-specialized open-weight large language model built on the NVIDIA Nemotron-3 sparse Mixture-of-Experts architecture. Training employs a three-phase easy-to-hard curriculum with continuous anti-forgetting anchoring, followe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, context, research, fine-tuning, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ReAD: Reinforcement-Guided Capability Distillation for Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.11290v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Capability distillation applies knowledge distillation to selected model capabilities, aiming to compress a large language model (LLM) into a smaller one while preserving the abilities needed for a downstream task. However, most existing methods treat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, ICL, context, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Where Reliability Lives in Vision-Language Models: A Mechanistic Study of Attention, Hidden States, and Causal Circuits
item.description: arXiv:2605.08200v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A pervasive intuition holds that vision-language models (VLMs) are most trustworthy when their attention maps look sharp: concentrated attention on the queried region should imply a confident, calibrated answer. We test this Attention-Confidence Assum...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, attention, study, RAG, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Spatial Priming Outperforms Semantic Prompting: A Grid-Based Approach to Improving LLM Accuracy on Chart Data Extraction
item.description: arXiv:2605.08220v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The automated extraction of data from scientific charts is a critical task for large-scale literature analysis. While multimodal Large Language Models (LLMs) show promise, their accuracy on non-standardized charts remains a challenge. This raises a ke...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, context, multimodal, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MemQ: Integrating Q-Learning into Self-Evolving Memory Agents over Provenance DAGs
item.description: arXiv:2605.08374v2 Announce Type: new Abstract: Episodic memory allows LLM agents to accumulate and retrieve experience, but current methods treat each memory independently, i.e., evaluating retrieval quality in isolation without accounting for the dependency chains through which memories enable th...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, memory, context, multimodal, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SkillLens: Adaptive Multi-Granularity Skill Reuse for Cost-Efficient LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.08386v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Skill libraries have become a practical way for LLM agents to reuse procedural experience across tasks. However, existing systems typically treat skills as flat, single-resolution prompt blocks. This creates a tension between relevance and cost: injec...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, context, framework, analysis, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LEAP: Unlocking dLLM Parallelism via Lookahead Early-Convergence Token Detection
item.description: arXiv:2605.10980v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diffusion Language Models (dLLMs) have garnered significant attention for their potential in highly parallel processing. The parallel capabilities of existing dLLMs stem from the assumption of conditional independence at high confidence levels, which ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, context, attention, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Interpretable EEG Microstate Discovery via Variational Deep Embedding: A Systematic Architecture Search with Multi-Quadrant Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2605.10947v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: EEG microstate analysis segments continuous brain electrical activity into brief, quasi-stable topographic configurations that reflect discrete functional brain states. Conventional approaches such as Modified K-Means operate directly in electrode spa...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, template, embedding, analysis, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: QuIDE: Mastering the Quantized Intelligence Trade-off via Active Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2605.10959v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: There is currently no unified metric for evaluating the efficiency of quantized neural networks. We propose QuIDE, built around the Intelligence Index I = (C x P)/log_2(T+1), which collapses the compression-accuracy-latency trade-off into a single sco...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, compression, image, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Hierarchical Multi-Scale Graph Neural Networks: Scalable Heterophilous Learning with Oversmoothing and Oversquashing Mitigation
item.description: arXiv:2605.10975v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Graphs with heterophily, where adjacent nodes carry different labels, are prevalent in real-world applications, from social networks to molecular interactions. However, existing spectral Graph Neural Network (GNN) approaches tailored for heterophilous...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, embedding, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: $\xi$-DPO: Direct Preference Optimization via Ratio Reward Margin
item.description: arXiv:2605.10981v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reference-free preference optimization has emerged as an efficient alternative to reinforcement learning from human feedback, with Simple Preference Optimization(SimPO) demonstrating strong performance by eliminating the explicit reference model throu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, analysis, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Structural Interpretations of Protein Language Model Representations via Differentiable Graph Partitioning
item.description: arXiv:2605.10985v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Protein language models such as ESM-2 learn rich residue representations that achieve strong performance on protein function prediction, but their features remain difficult to interpret as structural $\&$ evolutionary signals are encoded in dense late...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, analysis, arxiv, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
